content:
200	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
201	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
202	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
203	\[
204	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
205	  \qquad
206	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
207	\]
208	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
209	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
210	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
211	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
212	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
213	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
214	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
215	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
216	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
217	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
218	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
219	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
220	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
221	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
222	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
223	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
224	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
225	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
226	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
227	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
228	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
229	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
230	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
231	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
232	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
233	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
234	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
235	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
236	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
237	
238	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
239	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
240	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
241	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
242	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
243	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
244	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
245	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
246	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
247	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
248	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
249	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
250	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
251	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
252	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
253	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
254	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
255	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
256	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
257	
258	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
259	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
260	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
261	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
262	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
263	
264	\begin{definition}\leanok
265	[Arrow of an affine cover]
266	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
267	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
268	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
269	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
270	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
271	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
272	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
273	  apply.
274	\end{definition}
275	
276	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
277	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
278	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
279	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
280	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
281	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
282	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
283	  \[
284	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
285	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
286	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
287	  \]
288	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
289	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
290	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
291	\end{definition}
292	
293	\begin{definition}\leanok
294	[Push--pull object map]
295	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
296	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
297	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
298	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
299	  \[
300	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
301	  \]
302	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
303	\end{definition}
304	
305	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
306	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
308	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
309	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
310	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
311	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
312	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
313	  \[
314	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
315	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
316	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
317	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
318	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
319	      \;=\;
320	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
321	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
322	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
323	  \]
324	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
325	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
326	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
327	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
328	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
329	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
330	\end{definition}
331	
332	\begin{lemma}
333	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
334	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
335	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
336	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
337	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
338	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
339	\end{lemma}
340	
341	\begin{proof}
342	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
343	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
344	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
345	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
346	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
347	\end{proof}
348	
349	\begin{lemma}
350	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
351	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
352	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
353	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
354	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
355	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
356	  \[
357	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
358	  \]
359	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
360	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
361	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
362	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
363	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
364	\end{lemma}
365	
366	\begin{proof}
367	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
368	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
369	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
370	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
371	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
372	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
373	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
374	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
375	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
376	\end{proof}
377	
378	\begin{lemma}
379	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
380	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
381	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
382	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
383	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
384	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
385	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
386	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
387	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
388	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
389	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
390	  \[
391	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
392	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
393	    \;=\;
394	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
395	  \]
396	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
397	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
398	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
399	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
400	\end{lemma}
401	
402	\begin{proof}
403	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
404	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
405	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
406	\end{proof}
407	
408	\begin{lemma}
409	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
410	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
411	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
412	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
413	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
414	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
415	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
416	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
417	  collapses to the transport-light form
418	  \[
419	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
420	      \;\circ\; e_2
421	    \;=\;
422	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
423	      \;\circ\; e_3,
424	  \]
425	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
426	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
427	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
428	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
429	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
430	  composition (pentagon) law.
431	\end{lemma}
432	
433	\begin{proof}
434	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
435	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
436	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
437	\end{proof}
438	
439	\begin{definition}
440	[Push--pull functor]
441	  \label{def:push_pull_functor}
442	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor}
443	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
444	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
445	  \[
446	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
447	  \]
448	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
449	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
450	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
451	  laws established above: the identity law
452	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
453	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
454	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
455	\end{definition}
456	
457	\begin{definition}
458	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
459	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}
460	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial}
461	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
462	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
463	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
464	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
465	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
466	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection
467	  pushforwards \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\). This is the
468	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of
469	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} augments by the structure map \(\mathcal{F} \to
470	  \mathcal{C}^0\).
471	\end{definition}
472	
473	\begin{definition}\leanok
474	[Relative {\v C}ech complex from a nerve]
475	  \label{def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
476	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativeCechComplexOfNerve}
477	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
478	  Given \(f : X \to S\) and an augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
479	  \(N\), the \emph{relative {\v C}ech cochain complex} in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)
480	  obtained by forgetting the augmentation of \(N\), pushing the resulting
481	  cosimplicial object forward degreewise along \(f\), and taking the
482	  alternating-coface-map cochain complex. This passage uses only the preadditivity
483	  of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) and \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence; it is
484	  the coherence-free plumbing (3) that turns the nerve of
485	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.
486	\end{definition}

514	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
515	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
516	
517	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
518	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
519	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
520	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
521	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
522	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
523	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
524	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
525	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
526	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
527	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
528	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
529	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
530	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
531	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
532	
533	\begin{definition}
534	
535	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
536	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
537	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized,
538	    AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_sprod,
539	    AlgebraicGeometry.iInf_fin_succ,
540	    AlgebraicGeometry.qcohRestriction_eq_comparison,
541	    AlgebraicGeometry.tP,
542	    AlgebraicGeometry.tU,
543	    AlgebraicGeometry.phiL,
544	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi,
545	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi_eq_phiL,
546	    AlgebraicGeometry.restr_bridge}
547	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
548	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
549	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
550	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
551	  % as an $R_f$-module.
552	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
553	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
554	  % are the maps
555	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
556	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
557	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
558	  items (4)--(5).}
559	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
560	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
561	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
562	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
563	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
564	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
565	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
566	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
567	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
568	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
569	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
570	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
571	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
572	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
573	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
574	
575	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
576	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
577	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
578	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
579	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
580	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
581	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
582	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
583	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
584	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
585	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
586	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
587	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
588	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
589	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
590	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
591	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
592	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
593	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
594	
595	  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
596	  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
597	  intersection identity
598	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
599	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
600	  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
601	  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
602	  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
603	  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
604	  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
605	  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
606	  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
607	  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
608	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
609	  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
610	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
611	  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
612	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.
613	
614	  The identification is packaged by a few helpers: the two localised-module endpoints
615	  \(\operatorname{tP}, \operatorname{tU}\) (the section groups
616	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) named as away localisations at the two indices
617	  involved in a face), the per-\(\sigma\) comparison
618	  \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\) realised both as the bare
619	  localisation comparison \(\operatorname{phiL}\) and as its sheaf-level avatar
620	  \(\operatorname{phi}\) (their agreement is \(\operatorname{phi\_eq\_phiL}\)), and the
621	  accessor-bridge \(\operatorname{restr\_bridge}\) that exhibits the presheaf
622	  restriction map through these comparisons.
623	\end{definition}
624	
625	\begin{lemma}
626	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
627	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
628	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
629	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
630	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
631	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
632	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
633	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
634	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
635	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
636	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
637	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
638	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
639	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
640	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
641	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
642	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
643	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
644	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
645	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
646	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
647	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
648	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
649	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
650	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
651	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
652	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
653	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
654	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
655	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
656	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
657	  % L58--67).
658	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
659	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
660	  % is identified with the complex
661	  % $$
662	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
663	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
664	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
665	  % \ldots
666	  % $$"
667	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
668	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
669	  complex of localisations).}
670	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
671	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
672	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
673	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
674	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
675	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
676	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
677	  \[
678	    D^\bullet : \quad
679	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
680	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
681	    \cdots,
682	    \qquad
683	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
684	  \]
685	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
686	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
687	  \[
688	    1 \leq p
689	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
690	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
691	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
692	  \]
693	  which is the form the affine vanishing
694	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
695	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
696	  homomorphisms,
697	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
698	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
699	
700	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
701	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
702	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
703	  product}
704	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
705	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
706	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
707	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
708	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
709	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
710	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
711	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
712	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
713	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
714	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
715	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
716	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
717	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
718	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
719	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
720	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
721	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
722	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
723	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
724	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
725	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
726	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
727	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
728	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
729	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
730	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
731	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
732	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
733	\end{lemma}
734	
735	\begin{proof}
736	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
737	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
738	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
739	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
740	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
741	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
742	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
743	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
744	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
745	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
746	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
747	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
748	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
749	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
750	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
751	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
752	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
753	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
754	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
755	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
756	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
757	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
758	  element-level product equivalence
759	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
760	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
761	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
762	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
763	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
764	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
765	
766	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
767	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
768	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
769	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
770	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
771	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
772	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
773	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
774	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
775	  being proved.
776	\end{proof}
777	
778	\begin{lemma}
779	
780	[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
781	  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
782	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv,
783	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
784	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdAddEquiv}
785	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
786	  Let \(\{F_\sigma\}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}\) be the finite family of
787	  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
788	  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
789	  an equivalence of underlying types
790	  \[
791	    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
792	    \;\simeq\;
793	    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
794	  \]
795	  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
796	  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
797	  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
798	  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
799	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
800	  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
801	  this equivalence each coordinate
802	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)))\) is identified with the away
803	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
804	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
805	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
806	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
807	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
808	  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
809	  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
810	  Its packaging as an additive isomorphism --- the degreewise vertical map of the
811	  ladder transport in Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact} --- is recorded as
812	  \(\operatorname{sectionProdAddEquiv}\), the \(\operatorname{AddEquiv}\) underlying
813	  the coordinatewise comparison.
814	  The coordinate projection of the equivalence is recorded separately as the
815	  definitional restatement
816	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\): the \(\sigma\)-component of the
817	  image of \(y\) under the equivalence is the underlying group map of the categorical
818	  projection \(\pi_\sigma\) applied to \(y\).
819	\end{lemma}
820	
821	\begin{proof}
822	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
823	  The type-level equivalence is \(\operatorname{Concrete.productEquiv}\) applied to the
824	  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
825	  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
826	  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
827	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
828	  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
829	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
830	  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
831	  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
832	  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
833	  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
834	  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
835	  the stated identification.
836	\end{proof}
837	
838	\begin{lemma}
839	[Abstract cosimplicial unfold of the section {\v C}ech differential]
840	  \label{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
841	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_objD_apply,
842	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechFaceRestr}
843	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
844	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
845	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open cover. For a multi-index
846	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(q+2) \to I\) and a face index
847	  \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}(q+2)\), write
848	  \[
849	    \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i) :
850	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\Bigr)
851	      \;\longrightarrow\;
852	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)}\Bigr)
853	  \]
854	  for the \(i\)-th presheaf face restriction --- the value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the
855	  inclusion of opens
856	  \(\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)} \subseteq \bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\)
857	  obtained from the order-preserving coface \(d_i\) of the simplex category. Then,
858	  read through the product equivalence
859	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) of
860	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the underlying group action of the
861	  degree-\(q\) differential
862	  \(\operatorname{objD} q\) of the alternating-coface-map complex
863	  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}
864	  (\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\,\mathcal{U}\,\mathcal{F})\) is the alternating
865	  sum of the face restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates:
866	  \[
867	    \bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q+1}\,(\operatorname{objD} q\, t)\bigr)_\sigma
868	      \;=\;
869	      \sum_{i = 0}^{q+1} (-1)^i \,
870	        \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
871	          \Bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q}\, t\bigr)_{\sigma \circ d_i}\Bigr).
872	  \]
873	  This is \emph{purely cosimplicial}: it records the shape of the section {\v C}ech
874	  differential as an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions, with \emph{no}
875	  sheaf-theoretic or localisation identification of those restrictions. It is the
876	  abstract shape that the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
877	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} matches once the tilde-bridge
878	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} is supplied.
879	\end{lemma}
880	
881	\begin{proof}
882	  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
883	  Unfold the degree-\(q\) map of \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
884	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction \(\operatorname{objD} q\)
885	  is the alternating sum \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, d^i\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, each
886	  coface \(d^i\) being the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component
887	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
888	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
889	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
890	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
891	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
892	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
893	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
894	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
895	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
896	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
897	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
898	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
899	\end{proof}

3811	\section{Route B: the 01I8 counit isomorphism via section-localization}
3812	
3813	The unconditional 01I8 instance \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3814	\operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) is established here
3815	along the \emph{section-localization} route (Route B), the shortest path aligned with the ambient
3816	library's \(\widetilde{(-)} / \operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) layer. That layer states the entire
3817	local theory of \(\widetilde{(-)}\) in the language of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): the
3818	structure-sheaf-localization map \(\widetilde{(-)}.\operatorname{toOpen}\) exhibits
3819	\(\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M}) = M_f\) as a localization at the powers of \(f\), and the
3820	distinguished-open component of the counit \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) is \emph{literally} the
3821	localization lift \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.lift}\) of the section-restriction map along that
3822	identification. Route B therefore reduces 01I8 to a single keystone — that the section-restriction
3823	map \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) of a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
3824	localization at the powers of \(f\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule}) — and then checks
3825	the counit on the basis of distinguished opens. Compared with the alternative global-presentation
3826	route (Route A), Route B replaces that route's two genuine-mathematics obligations — the
3827	structure-sheaf base change \(\widetilde{M}|_{D(f)} = \widetilde{M_f}\)
3828	(Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_restrict_basicOpen}) and the finite-limit preservation of
3829	\(\widetilde{(-)}\) — with a single bounded categorical bridge, the \emph{restrict--over
3830	compatibility} of Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_over_compat} (step B3). The restriction-to-basic-open
3831	transport of Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen} (the affine restriction
3832	\(\operatorname{modulesRestrictBasicOpen}\) and its comparison isomorphism) is \emph{not} dormant on
3833	Route B: it is load-bearing inside the bridge B3 and the presentation transport B4. Only the
3834	structure-sheaf base-change Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_restrict_basicOpen} and the
3835	\(\widetilde{(-)}\)-exactness blocks remain below as a dormant Route-A fallback.
3836	
3837	The blocks immediately following record the Mathlib results Route B stands on — the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
3838	counit and its localization description, the essential-image criterion, the
3839	localization-uniqueness lemma, and the categorical primitives for transporting presentations across
3840	functors, isomorphisms and site equivalences — and then the local-model bricks and the B1--B6 chain
3841	that build the keystone; the keystone and the assembly carry out the route.
3842	
3843	\begin{lemma}[The tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit]
3844	  \label{lem:fromTildeGamma_mathlib}
3845	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}
3846	  \mathlibok
3847	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3848	  For a ring \(R\) and an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), the
3849	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
3850	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})} \to
3851	  \mathcal{F}\) is the natural morphism of sheaves of modules whose component over a distinguished
3852	  open \(D(f)\) is the localization lift \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.lift}\) of the
3853	  section-restriction map \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\)
3854	  along the structure-sheaf localization \(\widetilde{(-)}.\operatorname{toOpen}\) (the latter
3855	  exhibiting \(\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M}) = M_f\) as a localization at the powers of \(f\)).
3856	\end{lemma}
3857	
3858	\begin{lemma}[Criterion for the counit to be an isomorphism]
3859	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_iff_mathlib}
3860	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff}
3861	  \mathlibok
3862	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3863	  For an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), the counit
3864	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) is an isomorphism if and only if \(\mathcal{F}\) lies in the
3865	  essential image of the tilde functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\); equivalently
3866	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) for some \(R\)-module \(M\).
3867	\end{lemma}
3868	
3869	\begin{lemma}[The forgetful functor reflects isomorphisms]
3870	  \label{lem:forget_reflectsIso_mathlib}
3871	  \lean{SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget}
3872	  \mathlibok
3873	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3874	  The forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\) from sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to the
3875	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups is fully faithful, hence reflects isomorphisms; the same holds
3876	  for \(\operatorname{toSheaf}\). Consequently a morphism of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism as
3877	  soon as its underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups is, and — since a morphism of sheaves
3878	  which is an isomorphism on every member of a basis of the topology is an isomorphism — it suffices
3879	  to check \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) on the basis of distinguished opens \(\{D(f)\}\).
3880	\end{lemma}
3881	
3882	\begin{lemma}[Two localizations at the same set are canonically isomorphic]
3883	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearEquiv_mathlib}
3884	  \lean{IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv}
3885	  \mathlibok
3886	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3887	  Let \(S \subseteq R\) be a submonoid of a commutative ring and let \(\varphi : M \to M'\) and
3888	  \(\psi : M \to M''\) be \(R\)-linear maps that each exhibit their target as the localization of
3889	  \(M\) at \(S\) (i.e.\ \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule} S\, \varphi\) and
3890	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule} S\, \psi\)). Then there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
3891	  \(M' \cong M''\) compatible with \(\varphi, \psi\). In particular the localization lift
3892	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.lift} S\, \varphi\, \psi\) is an isomorphism whenever both
3893	  \(\varphi\) and \(\psi\) are localizations of \(M\) at \(S\).
3894	\end{lemma}
3895	
3896	% ===== Route B: Mathlib dependency anchors =====
3897	
3898	\begin{lemma}[The structure-sheaf localization of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(f)\)]
3899	  \label{lem:tilde_toOpen_isLocalizedModule_mathlib}
3900	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen}
3901	  \mathlibok
3902	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3903	  For \(M\) an \(R\)-module and \(f \in R\), Mathlib's map
3904	  \(\operatorname{toOpen} : M \to \Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M})\) exhibits \(\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M}) =
3905	  M_f\) as the localization of \(M\) at the powers of \(f\): it carries an
3906	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\) instance. Moreover the global component
3907	  \(\operatorname{toOpen}(M, \top)\) is an isomorphism (Mathlib's
3908	  \(\operatorname{isIso\_toOpen\_top}\)), identifying \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \widetilde{M}) =
3909	  M\).
3910	\end{lemma}
3911	
3912	\begin{lemma}[Transport of a presentation along a colimit-preserving functor]
3913	  \label{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3914	  \lean{SheafOfModules.Presentation.map}
3915	  \mathlibok
3916	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3917	  Let \(F : \operatorname{SheafOfModules} R \to \operatorname{SheafOfModules} S\) be a functor between
3918	  categories of sheaves of modules (possibly over different sites) that preserves colimits and carries
3919	  the unit object to the unit object, \(F(\mathcal{O}_R) \cong \mathcal{O}_S\). Then any presentation
3920	  of a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{M}\) — a right-exact sequence
3921	  \(\mathcal{O}_R^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}_R^{(I)} \to \mathcal{M} \to 0\) of generators and relations —
3922	  is carried by \(F\) to a presentation of \(F(\mathcal{M})\).
3923	\end{lemma}
3924	
3925	\begin{lemma}[Transport of a presentation across an isomorphism]
3926	  \label{lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib}
3927	  \lean{SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso}
3928	  \mathlibok
3929	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3930	  Let \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) be an isomorphism of sheaves of modules. A presentation of
3931	  \(\mathcal{M}\) induces a presentation of \(\mathcal{N}\), by composing its generators and relations
3932	  with \(\phi\).
3933	\end{lemma}
3934	
3935	\begin{lemma}[Site-equivalence transport of sheaves of modules]
3936	  \label{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}
3937	  \lean{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}
3938	  \mathlibok
3939	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3940	  Let \(e : \mathcal{C} \simeq \mathcal{D}\) be an equivalence of sites and let
3941	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{C}}\), \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{D}}\) be sheaves of rings related by
3942	  compatible comparison maps along \(e\). Then \(e\) induces an equivalence of categories of sheaves of
3943	  modules \(\operatorname{SheafOfModules} \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{C}} \simeq
3944	  \operatorname{SheafOfModules} \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{D}}\), compatible with the underlying
3945	  pushforwards. This is the engine that bridges two pictures of ``restriction to an open''.
3946	\end{lemma}
3947	
3948	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherence datum: a cover with a presentation on each member]
3949	  \label{lem:quasicoherentData_mathlib}
3950	  \lean{SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData}
3951	  \mathlibok
3952	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3953	  A \emph{quasi-coherence datum} for a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{F}\) consists of a family of objects
3954	  \(U_i\) of the site that covers the terminal object, together with, for each \(i\), a presentation of
3955	  the over-picture restriction \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} U_i\). A sheaf of modules is
3956	  quasi-coherent precisely when such a datum exists.
3957	\end{lemma}
3958	
3959	\begin{lemma}[Sections of a restricted module are sections over the image open]
3960	  \label{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
3961	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj}
3962	  \mathlibok
3963	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3964	  For an \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-module \(\mathcal{M}\), an open immersion \(\iota : X \to Y\), and an open
3965	  \(U \subseteq X\), the sections of the restricted module over \(U\) coincide definitionally with the
3966	  sections of \(\mathcal{M}\) over the image open \(\iota(U)\):
3967	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M}.\operatorname{restrict} \iota, U) = \Gamma(\mathcal{M}, \iota(U))\), and
3968	  likewise for the restriction maps. The section comparison is therefore an equality, not merely an
3969	  isomorphism.
3970	\end{lemma}

3974	\begin{lemma}[Section-restriction of \(\widetilde{M}\) localizes (local-model brick)]
3975	  \label{lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule}
3976	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_section_isLocalizedModule}
3977	  \uses{lem:tilde_toOpen_isLocalizedModule_mathlib}
3978	  Let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module and \(f \in R\). The section-restriction map
3979	  \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \widetilde{M}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M})\) of the associated
3980	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) exhibits its target as the localization of its source at the powers of
3981	  \(f\): one has \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\) for it.
3982	\end{lemma}
3983	\begin{proof}
3984	  \uses{lem:tilde_toOpen_isLocalizedModule_mathlib}
3985	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_toOpen_isLocalizedModule_mathlib} the distinguished-open component
3986	  \(\operatorname{toOpen} : M \to \Gamma(D(f), \widetilde{M})\) is a localization at the powers of
3987	  \(f\), and the global component \(M \to \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \widetilde{M})\) is an
3988	  isomorphism. Up to this global isomorphism, the section-restriction map is exactly
3989	  \(\operatorname{toOpen}\) over \(D(f)\); transporting the localization property along the
3990	  global-sections isomorphism that identifies \(M\) with \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
3991	  \widetilde{M})\) on the source shows that the section-restriction map is itself a localization at the
3992	  powers of \(f\).
3993	\end{proof}
3994	
3995	\begin{lemma}[Section-restriction localizes when the counit is an isomorphism (per-piece engine)]
3996	  \label{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeGamma}
3997	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
3998	  \uses{lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
3999	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module for which the
4000	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) is an isomorphism, and let \(f \in R\).
4001	  Then the section-restriction map \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f),
4002	  \mathcal{F})\) is a localization at the powers of \(f\).
4003	\end{lemma}
4004	\begin{proof}
4005	  \uses{lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
4006	  When \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) is an isomorphism, \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) with
4007	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}). The
4008	  counit isomorphism, pushed through the forgetful functor, identifies the section-restriction map of
4009	  \(\mathcal{F}\) with that of \(\widetilde{M}\) — compatibly on source and target by naturality. The
4010	  latter is a localization by Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule}; conjugating by the
4011	  isomorphism transports the localization property to the section-restriction map of \(\mathcal{F}\).
4012	\end{proof}
4013	
4014	\begin{lemma}[Section-restriction localizes for a globally presented module (keystone local case)]
4015	  \label{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation}
4016	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation}
4017	  \uses{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeGamma,
4018	    lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}
4019	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module that admits a global
4020	  presentation, and let \(f \in R\). Then the section-restriction map
4021	  \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) is a localization at the
4022	  powers of \(f\).
4023	\end{lemma}
4024	\begin{proof}
4025	  \uses{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeGamma,
4026	    lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}
4027	  A global presentation makes the counit \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) an isomorphism
4028	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}); now apply
4029	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeGamma}.
4030	\end{proof}

4034	\begin{lemma}[Finite presentation cover from quasi-coherence (Route B, step B1)]
4035	  \label{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}
4036	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}
4037	  \uses{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover, lem:quasicoherentData_mathlib}
4038	  % NOTE: to-build (Route B, step B1).
4039	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
4040	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then there exist a finite family \(g_1, \ldots, g_n \in R\) with
4041	  \(\operatorname{span}\{g_1, \ldots, g_n\} = R\) and, for each \(j\), a member \(U_{\varphi(j)}\) of
4042	  the quasi-coherence cover with \(D(g_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) carrying a presentation of the
4043	  over-picture restriction \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} U_{\varphi(j)}\).
4044	\end{lemma}
4045	\begin{proof}
4046	  \uses{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover, lem:quasicoherentData_mathlib}
4047	  Quasi-coherence supplies a quasi-coherence datum (Lemma~\ref{lem:quasicoherentData_mathlib}): a cover
4048	  \((U_i)_i\) of \(X\) together with a presentation of \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} U_i\) for each
4049	  \(i\). The cover-of-terminal-object condition translates to \(\bigcup_i U_i = \top\); feeding this to
4050	  the finite basic-open refinement (Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}) yields finitely
4051	  many \(g_j\) with \(D(g_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) and \(\operatorname{span}\{g_j\} = R\). The
4052	  required presentation of \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} U_{\varphi(j)}\) is the one attached to
4053	  \(U_{\varphi(j)}\) by the datum.
4054	\end{proof}

4056	\begin{lemma}[Restricting a presentation to a basic open (Route B, step B2)]
4057	  \label{lem:presentation_over_basicOpen}
4058	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.presentationOverBasicOpen}
4059	  \uses{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
4060	  % NOTE: to-build (Route B, step B2).
4061	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, let \(U\) be an open
4062	  carrying a presentation of the over-restriction \(\mathcal{M}.\operatorname{over} U\), and let
4063	  \(g \in R\) with \(D(g) \subseteq U\). Then \(\mathcal{M}.\operatorname{over} D(g)\) admits a
4064	  presentation.
4065	\end{lemma}
4066	\begin{proof}
4067	  \uses{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
4068	  The further over-restriction from \(U\) to the smaller open \(D(g)\) is a left adjoint, hence
4069	  preserves colimits, and carries the unit object to the unit object; the nested over-restriction
4070	  instances make it a functor between the corresponding categories of sheaves of modules. Transport the
4071	  given presentation of \(\mathcal{M}.\operatorname{over} U\) along this functor by
4072	  Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib} to obtain a presentation of
4073	  \(\mathcal{M}.\operatorname{over} D(g)\). This step is independent of the bridge of step B3.
4074	\end{proof}

4076	\begin{lemma}[Restrict--over compatibility (Route B, step B3 --- the bridge)]
4077	  \label{lem:restrict_over_compat}
4078	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.overBasicOpenIsoRestrict}
4079	  \uses{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
4080	    lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
4081	  % NOTE: to-build (Route B, step B3). THE load-bearing bridge of Route B.
4082	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module and \(g \in R\). Then the
4083	  over-picture restriction \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} D(g)\) — a sheaf of modules over the
4084	  localized site \((\operatorname{Opens} \operatorname{Spec} R).\operatorname{over} D(g)\), where the
4085	  quasi-coherence presentation lives — is isomorphic to the honest
4086	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R_g).\mathrm{Modules}\)-object \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)} =
4087	  \operatorname{modulesRestrictBasicOpen} g\, \mathcal{F}\), where the
4088	  \(\widetilde{(-)} / \operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) machinery lives, as sheaves of modules over the
4089	  identification \(D(g) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_g\).
4090	\end{lemma}
4091	\begin{proof}
4092	  \uses{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
4093	    lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
4094	  Both objects describe ``\(\mathcal{F}\) restricted to \(D(g)\)'', but in two different pictures: the
4095	  over-picture indexes the opens of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) lying under \(D(g)\), whereas
4096	  \(\operatorname{modulesRestrictBasicOpen} g\, \mathcal{F}\) is a sheaf on the affine scheme
4097	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_g\) via the comparison \(D(g) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_g\)
4098	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen}). The site equivalence
4099	  \((\operatorname{Opens} \operatorname{Spec} R).\operatorname{over} D(g) \simeq
4100	  \operatorname{Opens}(\operatorname{Spec} R_g)\) — the open-subscheme identification of opens, with its
4101	  structure-sheaf compatibility — induces, via
4102	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}, an equivalence of the two categories of
4103	  sheaves of modules whose object correspondence sends \(\mathcal{F}.\operatorname{over} D(g)\) to
4104	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\); on sections the comparison is the definitional equality of
4105	  Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}. This obligation is genuinely distinct from the structure-sheaf
4106	  base change \(\widetilde{M}|_{D(f)} = \widetilde{M_f}\)
4107	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_restrict_basicOpen}) and is \emph{not} discharged by the comparison isomorphism
4108	  of Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen}, which relates two scheme-picture restrictions and
4109	  never mentions the over-picture.
4110	\end{proof}

3927	  \lean{SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso}

4254	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) at the powers of \(f\). By the keystone

4256	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) at the powers of \(f\); hence both the source and the target of

4348	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4349	[Free \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules are quasi-coherent]
4350	  \label{lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
4351	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.free_isQuasicoherent}
4352	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma
4353	  % \texttt{lemma-colimit-quasi-coherent} (read from
4354	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1434--1438).
4355	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The direct sum of an arbitrary collection of quasi-coherent sheaves
4356	  % on $X$ is quasi-coherent. The same holds for colimits."
4357	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-colimit-quasi-coherent}.}
4358	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\). For any index type \(\iota\) the free
4359	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(\iota)}\) on \(\iota\) generators is
4360	  quasi-coherent. Indeed it is the sheaf \(\widetilde{R^{(\iota)}}\) associated to the
4361	  free \(R\)-module \(R^{(\iota)} = \bigoplus_{\iota} R\) (the finitely-supported
4362	  functions \(\iota \to R\)): the tilde functor carries \(R^{(\iota)}\) to a direct sum
4363	  of copies of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X = \widetilde{R}\), and every sheaf of
4364	  the form \(\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. Quasi-coherence is preserved under the
4365	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(\iota)} \cong \widetilde{R^{(\iota)}}\); concretely it is
4366	  obtained from the identification of the free \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module with the
4367	  tilde of the free \(R\)-module.

4370	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4371	[Finite basic-open refinement of a cover of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)]
4372	  \label{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
4373	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
4374	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-standard-open} (parts 2--3) and
4375	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
4376	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L531--534, L1289--1301).
4377	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\item Any open covering of $D(f)$ can be refined to a finite
4378	  % open covering of the form $D(f) = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(g_i)$.
4379	  % \item If $g_1, \ldots, g_n \in R$, then $D(f) \subset \bigcup D(g_i)$
4380	  % if and only if $g_1, \ldots, g_n$ generate the unit ideal in $R_f$."
4381	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
4382	  % $X$ is locally quasi-compact, i.e., every point has a fundamental
4383	  % system of quasi-compact neighbourhoods ...
4384	  % In other words, we get an open covering by $U$'s with this property.
4385	  % By Lemma \ref{lemma-standard-open} for example we can refine this
4386	  % covering to  a standard open covering."
4387	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-standard-open} and
4388	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof).}
4389	  Let \(R\) be a ring and \((U_i)_{i \in \iota}\) a family of opens of
4390	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) with \(\bigsqcup_i U_i = \operatorname{Spec} R\) (i.e.\
4391	  \(\bigcup_i U_i = \top\)). Then there exist finitely many elements
4392	  \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in R\) and indices \(\varphi : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to \iota\) such
4393	  that each basic open satisfies \(D(f_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) and the \(f_j\)
4394	  generate the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{span}\{f_1, \ldots, f_n\} = R\) (equivalently
4395	  \(\bigcup_j D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R\)).
4396	\end{lemma}
4397	\begin{proof}
4398	
4399	\leanok
4400	  The basic opens \(D(f)\), \(f \in R\), form a basis of the Zariski topology on
4401	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\); hence every \(U_i\) is a union of basic opens, and refining
4402	  the cover \((U_i)\) yields a cover of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by basic opens
4403	  \(D(f)\), each contained in some \(U_{\varphi}\). The space \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)
4404	  is quasi-compact, so finitely many of these basic opens \(D(f_1), \ldots, D(f_n)\)
4405	  already cover \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), with \(D(f_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) by
4406	  construction. Finally a finite family of basic opens covers all of
4407	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) if and only if the \(f_j\) generate the unit ideal:
4408	  \(\bigcup_j D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R \iff \operatorname{span}\{f_j\} = R\), since
4409	  the closed complement \(V(\operatorname{span}\{f_j\})\) is empty exactly when the ideal
4410	  is the whole ring.
4411	\end{proof}

4551	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, let
4552	  \(U \subseteq X\) be open, and suppose \(\mathcal{F}|_U\) admits a presentation
4553	  \(\mathcal{O}_U^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}_U^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}|_U \to 0\). Then for every
4554	  basic open \(D(g) \subseteq U\), the restricted module
4555	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) — viewed as an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R_g}\)-module via
4556	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen} — admits a presentation
4557	  \(\mathcal{O}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}|_{D(g)} \to 0\) over
4558	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_g\); in particular \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) is quasi-coherent over
4559	  \(R_g\).

4566	  \(D(g) \hookrightarrow U\) is an additive functor that preserves the structure sheaf, hence
4567	  preserves cokernels and arbitrary direct sums of \(\mathcal{O}\); applying it to the given
4568	  right-exact sequence yields a right-exact sequence
4569	  \(\mathcal{O}_{D(g)}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}_{D(g)}^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}|_{D(g)} \to 0\).
4570	  Transporting along \(D(g) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_g\)
4571	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen}) turns the free modules
4572	  \(\mathcal{O}_{D(g)}^{(I)}\), \(\mathcal{O}_{D(g)}^{(J)}\) into the free
4573	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R_g}\)-modules \(\widetilde{R_g}^{(I)}\),
4574	  \(\widetilde{R_g}^{(J)}\) (the structure sheaf restricts to the structure sheaf, and free
4575	  modules restrict to free modules by Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_restrict_basicOpen}). This is a
4576	  presentation of \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) over \(\operatorname{Spec} R_g\), exhibiting it as

4629	\begin{lemma}[\(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) is local on a finite spanning cover]
4630	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover}
4631	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover}
4632	  % NOTE: private helpers, internal only. The Lean proof uses several file-private helper
4633	  % lemmas (exists_sum_pow_eq_one, mem_range_of_span_pow, eq_zero_of_span_pow, map_smul_endFun,
4634	  % bump_eq, per_j_surj, per_j_eq) that cannot be referenced by fully-qualified name.
4635	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(M\) and \(N\) be \(R\)-modules, let
4636	  \(g : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-linear map, let \(f \in R\), and let
4637	  \(s : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to R\) be a finite family with
4638	  \(\operatorname{span}\{s_1, \ldots, s_n\} = R\) (the unit ideal). For each \(j\) write
4639	  \(M_{s_j} = (\operatorname{powers} s_j)^{-1} M\) and \(N_{s_j} = (\operatorname{powers}
4640	  s_j)^{-1} N\) for the localisations at the powers of \(s_j\), and let
4641	  \(g_{s_j} : M_{s_j} \to N_{s_j}\) be the induced \(R\)-linear (equivalently
4642	  \(R_{s_j}\)-linear) map. Suppose that for every \(j\) the localised map \(g_{s_j}\)
4643	  exhibits \(N_{s_j}\) as the localisation of \(M_{s_j}\) at the powers of (the image of)
4644	  \(f\), i.e.\ \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\). Then
4645	  \(g\) itself exhibits \(N\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the powers of \(f\):
4646	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\).
4647	\end{lemma}
4648	\begin{proof}
4649	
4650	  This is a purely commutative-algebra statement: the predicate
4651	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\) is the conjunction of its
4652	  three defining conditions, and each is verified by descent along the spanning cover. Throughout we use the standard partition
4653	  of unity: since \(\operatorname{span}\{s_j\} = R\), for any chosen exponents \(N_j\) the
4654	  powers \(s_j^{N_j}\) again span the unit ideal, so there are \(r_j \in R\) with
4655	  \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{N_j} = 1\); and an element of an \(R\)-module is zero iff its image in
4656	  \(M_{s_j}\) vanishes for every \(j\) (locality of being zero on a spanning cover, the
4657	  \(s_j\)-torsion form of the same partition of unity).
4658	
4659	  \emph{\(f\) acts invertibly on \(N\).} For each \(j\), the hypothesis
4660	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\) makes multiplication
4661	  by \(f\) on \(N_{s_j}\) bijective. Multiplication by \(f\) on \(N\) is an isomorphism iff it
4662	  is so after localising at each \(s_j\) (an \(R\)-linear endomorphism is bijective iff it is
4663	  bijective on the cover \(\{D(s_j)\}\), since injectivity and surjectivity are both local on
4664	  a spanning cover); hence \(f \cdot (-) : N \to N\) is bijective, and likewise every power
4665	  \(f^k\).
4666	
4667	  \emph{Every \(y \in N\) satisfies \(f^k y = g(x)\) for some \(x \in M\), \(k\).}
4668	  Fix \(y \in N\). For each \(j\), surjectivity of \(g_{s_j}\) as a localisation at the powers
4669	  of \(f\) yields \(m_j \in M\), exponents \(a_j\) (clearing the \(s_j\)-denominator) and
4670	  \(k_j\) with \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k_j} y = g(m_j)\) holding after localising at \(s_j\); clearing
4671	  the remaining \(s_j\)-torsion (enlarging \(a_j\)) we may take this as an honest equality
4672	  \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k_j} y = g(m_j)\) in \(N\). Choose a common \(k = \max_j k_j\) (replacing
4673	  \(m_j\) by \(f^{k - k_j} m_j\)) so that \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k} y = g(m_j)\) for all \(j\). Pick,
4674	  by the partition of unity, \(r_j\) with \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{a_j} = 1\) (possible because the
4675	  \(s_j^{a_j}\) span the unit ideal). Then
4676	  \(f^{k} y = \sum_j r_j s_j^{a_j} f^{k} y = \sum_j r_j\, g(m_j) = g\bigl(\sum_j r_j m_j\bigr)\),
4677	  so \(x = \sum_j r_j m_j\) and the exponent \(k\) work.
4678	
4679	  \emph{If \(g(x) = g(x')\) then \(f^k(x - x') = 0\) for some \(k\).} Put
4680	  \(z = x - x'\), so \(g(z) = 0\). For each \(j\), the equaliser clause of
4681	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\) gives, from the
4682	  vanishing of \(g_{s_j}(z) = 0\) in \(N_{s_j}\), an exponent \(k_j\) with
4683	  \(f^{k_j} z = 0\) after localising at \(s_j\); clearing the \(s_j\)-denominator there is
4684	  \(b_j\) with \(s_j^{b_j} f^{k_j} z = 0\) in \(M\). Take \(k = \max_j k_j\), so
4685	  \(s_j^{b_j} f^{k} z = 0\) for all \(j\). With \(r_j\) chosen so that
4686	  \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{b_j} = 1\),
4687	  \(f^{k} z = \sum_j r_j s_j^{b_j} f^{k} z = 0\). Hence \(f^{k}(x - x') = 0\).
4688	
4689	  The three clauses are exactly the data of
4690	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\).
4691	\end{proof}
4692

3734	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01HV, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves}.}
3735	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3736	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Writing \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global sections,
3737	  there is a natural isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
3738	  under which \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F}) = M_f\) for every \(f \in R\); in particular the sections of
3739	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over a distinguished open \(D(f)\) are the away localisation \(M_f\). This is the
3740	  affine quasi-coherent structure theorem (Tag 01HV, with the equivalence quasi-coherent-on-affine
3741	  \(\simeq\) \(R\)-modules, Tag 01I8).
